_meta.note: Normalized obligation matrix derived from data/global-frameworks.json. All times in hours. breach_notification.hours is null for jurisdictions that require notification only on a discretionary trigger.

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach affecting data subject rights
breach_notification.authority: European Data Protection Board (EDPB)

breach_notification.trigger: Significant incident (substantial impact on service provision)
breach_notification.authority: ENISA + Member State NCAs

breach_notification.trigger: Major ICT-related incident (RTO/RPO breach, high financial impact, reputational damage)
breach_notification.authority: ESAs (EBA, EIOPA, ESMA)

breach_notification.trigger: Actively exploited vulnerability discovered in the product
breach_notification.authority: ENISA + National Market Surveillance Authorities

patch_sla.note: 14 days for critical patches (internet-facing). Better than NIST 30-day, worse than ASD 48h.
ISM-1623: 48h for critical with existing exploit. Best non-CISA-aware SLA of any major framework.
Maturity Level 3 — 48h for extreme risk. ML1 = 1 month, ML2 = 2 weeks, ML3 = 48h.

breach_notification.trigger: Material information security incident
breach_notification.authority: Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA)

breach_notification.trigger: Significant IT disruption (> 30min customer impact), security breach
breach_notification.authority: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS)

patch_sla.note: 1 month for critical patches — same PCI problem. Risk-based timeline in practice.

breach_notification.trigger: Cybersecurity incident affecting CII
breach_notification.authority: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA)

breach_notification.trigger: Leak, loss, or damage of personal data likely to harm individual rights (specifically: special-care-required PI, breaches likely to cause financial damage, intentional acts, or >1,000 affected individuals)
breach_notification.authority: Personal Information Protection Commission (PPC, 個人情報保護委員会)

APPI.authority: Personal Information Protection Commission (PPC, 個人情報保護委員会)

breach_notification.trigger: Mandatory incident reporting categories (data breach, ransomware, website defacement, etc.)
breach_notification.authority: Computer Emergency Response Team — India (CERT-In)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach affecting Data Principals
breach_notification.authority: Data Protection Board of India (DPBI)

breach_notification.trigger: Technology or cyber incident with material operational impact
breach_notification.authority: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI)

OSFI_B10.authority: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI)

breach_notification.trigger: Confidentiality incident presenting a 'risk of serious injury' must be reported to the CAI and affected individuals 'with diligence'
breach_notification.authority: Commission d'accès à l'information du Québec (CAI)

QC_LAW_25.authority: Commission d'accès à l'information du Québec (CAI)

breach_notification.trigger: Breach of security safeguards involving personal information where it is reasonable to believe there is a 'real risk of significant harm'
breach_notification.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC)

PIPEDA.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC)

breach_notification.trigger: Security incident that may cause relevant risk or damage to data subjects
breach_notification.authority: Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados (ANPD)

LGPD.authority: Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados (ANPD)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal information leak, tampering, or loss
breach_notification.authority: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) — co-regulating with MIIT, MPS, SAMR

PIPL.authority: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) — co-regulating with MIIT, MPS, SAMR

breach_notification.trigger: Data security incident; immediate disposal + notification to competent authority
breach_notification.authority: CAC, MPS, MSS — sectoral regulators with national security coordination

DSL.authority: CAC, MPS, MSS — sectoral regulators with national security coordination

breach_notification.trigger: Compromise of personal information where reasonable grounds to believe the information has been accessed or acquired by unauthorised person
breach_notification.authority: Information Regulator (South Africa)

breach_notification.trigger: Breach or violation of personal data that would prejudice privacy, confidentiality, or security of the data subject
breach_notification.authority: UAE Data Office (Federal); TDRA coordinates digital sector; sectoral regulators apply in finance, health

UAE_PDPL.authority: UAE Data Office (Federal); TDRA coordinates digital sector; sectoral regulators apply in finance, health

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach that may cause damage to personal data or to the data subject
breach_notification.authority: Saudi Data and AI Authority (SDAIA)

breach_notification.trigger: Notifiable privacy breach: unauthorised access, disclosure, loss of personal information that has caused or is likely to cause serious harm
breach_notification.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner (OPC) — Te Mana Mātāpono Matatapu

PRIVACY_ACT_2020.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner (OPC) — Te Mana Mātāpono Matatapu

breach_notification.trigger: Personal information leakage
breach_notification.authority: Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC)

breach_notification.trigger: Security breach affecting personal data with risk to data subject rights
breach_notification.authority: Agencia de Protección de Datos Personales (APDP) — established by Law 21,719; pre-existing oversight by SERNAC and Civil Registry

CL_DPL.authority: Agencia de Protección de Datos Personales (APDP) — established by Law 21,719; pre-existing oversight by SERNAC and Civil Registry

breach_notification.trigger: Severe security incident affecting databases at medium or high security level (Regulation 11)
breach_notification.authority: Privacy Protection Authority (PPA, רשות הגנת הפרטיות); transitioning to 'Public Authority for Privacy Protection' under Amendment 13 with expanded enforcement powers

PPL_5741_1981.authority: Privacy Protection Authority (PPA, רשות הגנת הפרטיות); transitioning to 'Public Authority for Privacy Protection' under Amendment 13 with expanded enforcement powers

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach likely to result in a high risk to data subjects' personality or fundamental rights (Art. 24 revFADP)
breach_notification.authority: Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC, EDÖB)

REVFADP.authority: Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC, EDÖB)

breach_notification.trigger: Cyberattack on critical infrastructure operators (per Information Security Act Art. 74e, in force 2025-04-01)
breach_notification.authority: Federal Office for Cybersecurity (BACS, formerly NCSC-CH; previously MELANI)

NCSC_CH.authority: Federal Office for Cybersecurity (BACS, formerly NCSC-CH; previously MELANI)

breach_notification.trigger: Voluntary breach notification to PCPD recommended for breaches involving sensitive data or significant harm potential
breach_notification.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data (PCPD)

PDPO.authority: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data (PCPD)

breach_notification.trigger: Significant IT control failures or cyber events affecting customer services or risk profile
breach_notification.authority: Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data theft, alteration, damage, loss, or leakage (Art. 12)
breach_notification.authority: Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC, 個人資料保護委員會) — established under 2023 amendment; previously sectoral regulators

PDPA_TW.authority: Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC, 個人資料保護委員會) — established under 2023 amendment; previously sectoral regulators

breach_notification.trigger: Cyber incident affecting CIP critical information and communications systems
breach_notification.authority: Ministry of Digital Affairs (MoDA, 數位發展部) — established 2022-08-27; previously National Information and Communication Security Taskforce. Operational arm: National Institute of Cyber Security (NICS, 國家資通安全研究院)

CYBER_SECURITY_MGMT_ACT.authority: Ministry of Digital Affairs (MoDA, 數位發展部) — established 2022-08-27; previously National Information and Communication Security Taskforce. Operational arm: National Institute of Cyber Security (NICS, 國家資通安全研究院)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data protection failure causing data leakage, loss, or unauthorised processing (Art. 46)
breach_notification.authority: Personal Data Protection Agency (Lembaga Pelindungan Data Pribadi) — being established under Presidential Regulation pursuant to UU PDP Art. 58-60; interim oversight by Ministry of Communication and Informatics (Kominfo, now Komdigi)

PDP_LAW.authority: Personal Data Protection Agency (Lembaga Pelindungan Data Pribadi) — being established under Presidential Regulation pursuant to UU PDP Art. 58-60; interim oversight by Ministry of Communication and Informatics (Kominfo, now Komdigi)

breach_notification.trigger: Significant cyber incident affecting payment services or banking operations
breach_notification.authority: Bank Indonesia (BI); Financial Services Authority (Otoritas Jasa Keuangan, OJK)

BI_CYBER_REG.authority: Bank Indonesia (BI); Financial Services Authority (Otoritas Jasa Keuangan, OJK)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach causing risk to data subject rights and interests
breach_notification.authority: Ministry of Public Security (Bộ Công An) — Department of Cybersecurity and Hi-Tech Crime Prevention (A05)

PDPD.authority: Ministry of Public Security (Bộ Công An) — Department of Cybersecurity and Hi-Tech Crime Prevention (A05)

US_NYDFS.jurisdiction_name: United States — New York State (sub-national, financial services)

breach_notification.trigger: Cybersecurity event that has a reasonable likelihood of materially harming any material part of the normal operations of the Covered Entity OR a Cybersecurity Event involving Nonpublic Information that requires notice to a government body, self-regulatory agency, or other supervisory body (§500.17(a))
breach_notification.authority: New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS)

NYDFS_PART_500.authority: New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach affecting data subject rights (GDPR Art. 33 standard)
breach_notification.authority: Datatilsynet (Norwegian Data Protection Authority)

patch_sla.note: NSM Grunnprinsipper 2.2.5 recommends 'as soon as possible' for known-exploited vulnerabilities; sector guidance and audit practice operationalise this as ~30 days for critical, with accelerated handling for actively exploited cases.
patch_sla.authority: Nasjonal sikkerhetsmyndighet (NSM) — Norwegian National Security Authority

NSM_GRUNNPRINSIPPER.authority: Nasjonal sikkerhetsmyndighet (NSM) — Norwegian National Security Authority

breach_notification.trigger: Significant incident at essential/important entity (NIS2 standard)
breach_notification.authority: NSM as national CSIRT; Nkom (telecom), Finanstilsynet (finance), NVE (energy), Helsedirektoratet (health), and other sectoral authorities

NIS2_NORWAY.authority: NSM as national CSIRT; Nkom (telecom), Finanstilsynet (finance), NVE (energy), Helsedirektoratet (health), and other sectoral authorities

breach_notification.trigger: Security breach affecting rights of data subjects (Reglamento Art. 63)
breach_notification.authority: Until 2025: INAI (Instituto Nacional de Transparencia, Acceso a la Información y Protección de Datos Personales); reform in progress to transfer functions to a successor agency under the Secretaría Anticorrupción y Buen Gobierno (constitutional reform published 2024-12-20 dissolving INAI; transition law pending)

LFPDPPP.authority: Until 2025: INAI (Instituto Nacional de Transparencia, Acceso a la Información y Protección de Datos Personales); reform in progress to transfer functions to a successor agency under the Secretaría Anticorrupción y Buen Gobierno (constitutional reform published 2024-12-20 dissolving INAI; transition law pending)

breach_notification.trigger: Cyber incident affecting operations, services, or sensitive information
breach_notification.authority: Comisión Nacional Bancaria y de Valores (CNBV); CONDUSEF for consumer protection in financial services; Banco de México for payment systems oversight

CONDUSEF_CNBV_CYBER.authority: Comisión Nacional Bancaria y de Valores (CNBV); CONDUSEF for consumer protection in financial services; Banco de México for payment systems oversight

breach_notification.trigger: Security incident affecting personal data
breach_notification.authority: Agencia de Acceso a la Información Pública (AAIP) — Dirección Nacional de Protección de Datos Personales

LPDP.authority: Agencia de Acceso a la Información Pública (AAIP) — Dirección Nacional de Protección de Datos Personales

breach_notification.trigger: Cyber incident with significant impact on operations, customers or sensitive information
breach_notification.authority: Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA) — Superintendencia de Entidades Financieras y Cambiarias

BCRA_CYBER.authority: Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA) — Superintendencia de Entidades Financieras y Cambiarias

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach (data being acquired by unauthorised parties)
breach_notification.authority: Kişisel Verileri Koruma Kurumu (KVKK Authority / KVKK)

KVKK.authority: Kişisel Verileri Koruma Kurumu (KVKK Authority / KVKK)

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach that risks the rights and freedoms of persons
breach_notification.authority: Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC) — Office of the PDPC (PDPC Office)

PDPA_TH.authority: Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC) — Office of the PDPC (PDPC Office)

breach_notification.trigger: Cyber threat affecting CII (graduated severity)
breach_notification.authority: National Cyber Security Agency (NCSA) — สำนักงานคณะกรรมการการรักษาความมั่นคงปลอดภัยไซเบอร์แห่งชาติ

CSA_TH.authority: National Cyber Security Agency (NCSA) — สำนักงานคณะกรรมการการรักษาความมั่นคงปลอดภัยไซเบอร์แห่งชาติ

breach_notification.trigger: Personal data breach where (a) sensitive personal information or information that may enable identity fraud are reasonably believed to have been acquired by an unauthorised person, and (b) it is likely to give rise to a real risk of serious harm
breach_notification.authority: National Privacy Commission (NPC)

breach_notification.trigger: Major cyber incident or significant disruption to banking operations
breach_notification.authority: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP)

US_CALIFORNIA.jurisdiction_name: United States — California (sub-national, privacy + AI baseline)

breach_notification.trigger: California's separate breach-notification statute Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.82 applies to security breaches of personal information
breach_notification.authority: California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA) — primary; California Attorney General — concurrent enforcement

CCPA_CPRA.authority: California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA) — primary; California Attorney General — concurrent enforcement

sorted_by_breach_notification_hours.trigger: Cyber incident affecting CIP critical information and communications systems
Significant IT disruption (> 30min customer impact), security breach
Cybersecurity incident affecting CII
Major cyber incident or significant disruption to banking operations
Major ICT-related incident (RTO/RPO breach, high financial impact, reputational damage)
Mandatory incident reporting categories (data breach, ransomware, website defacement, etc.)
Significant incident (substantial impact on service provision)
Actively exploited vulnerability discovered in the product
Technology or cyber incident with material operational impact
Cyberattack on critical infrastructure operators (per Information Security Act Art. 74e, in force 2025-04-01)
Significant incident at essential/important entity (NIS2 standard)
Cyber incident affecting operations, services, or sensitive information
Cyber incident with significant impact on operations, customers or sensitive information
Personal data breach affecting data subject rights
Material information security incident
Personal data breach affecting Data Principals
Personal data breach that may cause damage to personal data or to the data subject
Personal information leakage
Security breach affecting personal data with risk to data subject rights
Personal data protection failure causing data leakage, loss, or unauthorised processing (Art. 46)
Personal data breach causing risk to data subject rights and interests

Cybersecurity event that has a reasonable likelihood of materially harming any material part of the normal operations of the Covered Entity OR a Cybersecurity Event involving Nonpublic Information that requires notice to a government body, self-regulatory agency, or other supervisory body (§500.17(a))
Personal data breach affecting data subject rights (GDPR Art. 33 standard)
Personal data breach (data being acquired by unauthorised parties)
Personal data breach that risks the rights and freedoms of persons

Personal data breach where (a) sensitive personal information or information that may enable identity fraud are reasonably believed to have been acquired by an unauthorised person, and (b) it is likely to give rise to a real risk of serious harm

sorted_by_patch_sla_hours.note: ISM-1623: 48h for critical with existing exploit. Best non-CISA-aware SLA of any major framework.
Maturity Level 3 — 48h for extreme risk. ML1 = 1 month, ML2 = 2 weeks, ML3 = 48h.
14 days for critical patches (internet-facing). Better than NIST 30-day, worse than ASD 48h.
1 month for critical patches — same PCI problem. Risk-based timeline in practice.

NSM Grunnprinsipper 2.2.5 recommends 'as soon as possible' for known-exploited vulnerabilities; sector guidance and audit practice operationalise this as ~30 days for critical, with accelerated handling for actively exploited cases.
